page: Vultr vhp-1c-1gb-intel is an entry-level virtual private server belonging to the High Performance family. It features a single shared vCPU based on the Intel Xeon Cascadelake architecture, operating at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The processor includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 4 MB of L2 cache, and 16 MB of L3 cache, supporting instruction sets such as AVX, AVX-512, and AES cryptography. This server is equipped with 1.0 GB of system memory and 25 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. It also includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results indicate that the server delivers average performance in single-core CPU tasks, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1421.08. However, due to its single-core configuration, multi-core performance is limited, scoring in the bottom 10% across several metrics. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with read-only large block speeds at 9.16 GB/sec and write large block speeds at 7.89 GB/sec. Passmark CPU and memory scores fall into the bottom 10%, reflecting the resource constraints of a single shared vCPU. Redis SET operations achieve 1.58 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput is measured at 1.37 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this server represents a low-cost entry point suitable for workloads with minimal resource requirements. The primary tradeoff is the shared CPU allocation and limited 1.0 GB memory capacity, which restricts its capability to handle concurrent processes or memory-intensive applications. This configuration is best suited for lightweight applications such as basic static web serving, small-scale development environments, simple cron jobs, or low-traffic utility services.
description: Vultr vhp-1c-1gb-intel is an entry-level virtual server featuring a single shared Intel Xeon Cascadelake vCPU operating at 2.0 GHz, paired with 1.0 GB of RAM and 25 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Built on the x86_64 architecture, the processor supports AVX-512 and AES instructions. Benchmark data shows average single-core CPU performance with a stress-ng score of 1421.08, though multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics fall into the bottom 10% due to the single-core, shared-resource design. This server offers a highly economical entry point for lightweight workloads. It is best suited for basic static web serving, small development environments, and simple utility scripts that do not require high memory or parallel processing capabilities.
